chosen_action.title: Stop executor from resurfacing post-hold-only publisher packets as do-now manual assets

why_this_action.summary: The current review window truth says there is no do-now handoff packet. The highest-leverage executable move was to patch the executor-side manual-asset enumerator so it stops surfacing the primary-repo-flat packet during short-window congestion after repeated prepared-only refreshes, which was undermining the execution-board hold contract and increasing the odds of fake follow-through on the post-hold path.

result.summary: Patched agents/marketing/distribution_lane_executor.py so _manual_outreach_assets_waiting_for_execution() now respects the short review-window hold and the prepared-only repeat threshold for the primary-repo-flat packet. Added a unit test that proves the executor now hides that packet until congestion clears instead of presenting it as an immediate manual asset.

expected_outcome: The scheduled post-hold rerun at 2026-05-27T14:26:29+02:00 will be less likely to surface a fake do-now publisher follow-through asset and more likely to choose either a genuinely executable lane or another concrete repair.

measurement_window.success_metric: Before or at the 2026-05-27T14:26:29+02:00 rerun, executor-side manual asset discovery no longer returns the current primary-repo-flat packet while short-window congestion is still active and the repeat threshold has already been hit.
measurement_window.kill_condition: If the next repair or rerun still surfaces that packet as a do-now manual asset before the short-window release, escalate into a wider selector/executor/manual-follow-through truth unification repair.
